proposed_experiment_variables.pitting_induction_time: The measured time delay (τ_ind) required for stable, localized pit nucleation to occur when the specimen is held at a constant anodic potential during potentiostatic testing.
proposed_experiment_variables.pit_number_density: The quantitative count or surface density of stable corrosion pits (n) formed on the exposed material surface at a specific applied potential, used to evaluate the extent of localized breakdown and extrapolate zero-pit potentials.
proposed_experiment_variables.critical_passivation_potential: The explicit electrochemical potential (Ekp) at which the active-to-passive transition occurs, marking the initial formation of the protective passive film prior to reaching the pitting potential.
proposed_experiment_variables.critical_passivation_current_density: The peak anodic current density (ikp) observed at the critical passivation potential, representing the maximum active dissolution rate immediately before the passive film stabilizes.
